---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-section">
  <div class="hero-photo">
    <img src="images/profile.jpg" alt="Nadika Sharma" />
  </div>
  <div class="hero-text">
    <h1>Nadika Sharma</h1>
    <p class="hero-tagline">Data Science Student · Storyteller with Data</p>
    <p>
      Hi, I'm Nadika! I'm a data science student with a passion for turning messy, 
      real-world data into meaningful insights. I love working at the intersection 
      of <strong>statistics, visualization, and storytelling</strong> — finding the 
      narrative hiding in numbers. Browse my projects below or 
      <a href="resume.qmd">view my resume</a> to learn more about my work.
    </p>
    <div class="social-links">
      <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/nadikasharma" target="_blank">
        <i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn
      </a>
      <a href="https://github.com/nadikasharma" target="_blank">
        <i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub
      </a>
      <a href="mailto:sharmanadka@gmail.com">
        <i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Email
      </a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="home-about">
  <div class="warm-divider"></div>
  <h2>A little more about me</h2>
  <p>
    I'm currently studying data science and enjoy exploring questions in 
    <strong>public health, environmental data, and social equity</strong>. 
    Outside of coursework, I enjoy hiking, cooking, and reading everything 
    from literary fiction to statistics textbooks. I'm always looking for 
    opportunities to apply data skills to problems that matter.
  </p>
  <p>
    This website is my living portfolio — updated throughout the term with 
    class projects, side projects, and anything else I'm learning. 
    Take a look around!
  </p>
</div>
```

**Things to update before using it:**
- Replace `sharma.nadika@example.com` with your real email
- Replace `nadikasharma` in the LinkedIn and GitHub links with your actual usernames
- Update the bio paragraph to reflect your real interests and focus areas
- Make sure your photo is saved as `images/profile.jpg` in the same folder
